tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is mildly impaired (elevated creatinine), with significant proteinuria and ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible renal pathology or dehydration. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, indicating no hyperglycemia. Recommend further evaluation for renal impairment and possible causes of proteinuria and ketonuria.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
